name: Canada

visas.name: Express Entry — Federal Skilled Worker
visas.description: Canada's main points-based route for skilled workers. Candidates enter a pool and receive an Invitation to Apply (ITA) based on their Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score. Leads directly to permanent residence.
requirements: At least 1 year of skilled work experience (NOC TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3)
Language test: CLB/NCLC 7 (English or French)
Educational credential assessment if degree was obtained outside Canada
CRS score competitive enough for a draw (varies; often 470–560+)
visas.leadsTo: Permanent Residency → Canadian citizenship after 3 years

visas.name: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP)
visas.description: Each Canadian province nominates skilled workers based on its own economic needs. Many streams — some are faster than Express Entry, some don't require a prior job offer.
requirements: Meet the specific province's stream requirements (varies widely)
Many streams require a job offer or ties to the province
Enhanced PNP streams require an active Express Entry profile
visas.leadsTo: Permanent Residency → Canadian citizenship after 3 years

visas.name: Spousal / Partner Sponsorship
visas.description: Canadian citizens or permanent residents can sponsor their spouse, common-law partner, or conjugal partner for permanent residence.
requirements: Sponsor is a Canadian citizen or PR, aged 18+
Genuine relationship (marriage certificate or proof of cohabitation)
Sponsor is financially self-sufficient
Neither party has an undisclosed previous sponsorship that ended in default
visas.leadsTo: Permanent Residency → Canadian citizenship after 3 years

visas.name: Study Permit
visas.description: Study at a Designated Learning Institution (DLI) in Canada. Most students can work up to 24 hours/week off-campus during the academic year.
requirements: Acceptance letter from a Designated Learning Institution (DLI)
Proof of financial support: CAD $20,635/year in living expenses + tuition
No criminal record; medical exam if required
Ties to your home country demonstrating intent to return (unless applying for PR)
visas.leadsTo: Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP) for 1–3 years → Express Entry pathway

visas.name: Start-Up Visa
visas.description: Permanent residence for innovative entrepreneurs who secure backing from a Canadian venture capital fund, angel investor group, or business incubator.
requirements: Letter of support from a designated Canadian organisation
Language requirements: CLB/NCLC 5
Sufficient settlement funds
Innovative business concept with the potential to be competitive internationally
visas.leadsTo: Permanent Residency → Canadian citizenship after 3 years

visas.name: International Experience Canada (Working Holiday)
visas.description: Allows young people (18–35) from eligible countries to work and travel in Canada for up to 2 years. Entry through a random draw from a pool.
requirements: Aged 18–35 (upper age limit varies by country)
Citizen of a country with an IEC bilateral agreement
CAD $2,500 in available funds
Health insurance for the duration of your stay
Clean criminal record
visas.leadsTo: Canadian work experience can boost your Express Entry CRS score

citizenship.name: Canadian Citizenship
citizenship.requiresResidency: 3 of the last 5 years as a permanent resident (1,095 days)

benefits.childBenefit: Canada Child Benefit (CCB) — up to CAD $7,787/year per child under 6; $6,570/year for 6–17 (2024)
benefits.healthInsurance: Provincial health plans — free for PRs and citizens; a waiting period (up to 3 months) may apply for new arrivals
benefits.unemployment: Employment Insurance (EI) — up to 55% of insurable earnings for up to 45 weeks

taxNotes: Canada taxes worldwide income for tax residents. Tax treaties with 90+ countries. TFSA (Tax-Free Savings Account) and RRSP (Registered Retirement Savings Plan) available to residents.
